summary: Verified P0 and P1 bug fixes for mobile UI/UX. All 5 pillar pages (Celebrate, Learn, Fit, Travel, Farewell) have mobile back buttons. Backend tests pass 100% (9/9). Service request endpoint correctly shows user name 'Dipali' (not 'Guest'). View Details button on product cards works and opens modal with PawMeter (Net Pawmoter Score™) visible.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All P0/P1 bugs verified as fixed: 1) Mobile back buttons present on Celebrate, Learn, Fit, Travel, Farewell pages (button with aria-label='Go back' and ChevronLeft icon), 2) Ask Concierge form pre-fills user data (name, phone, email) from AuthContext, 3) Pet name auto-populates when user has pets, 4) Service request endpoint shows correct user name 'Dipali' not 'Guest', 5) View Details button on product cards opens modal with Net Pawmoter Score™ (PawMeter) visible, 6) Pet selector in navbar allows switching between pets and stores selection in localStorage.

mobile_back_buttons.implementation: button with aria-label='Go back' containing ChevronLeft icon, positioned absolute top-4 left-4 with white/20 backdrop

ask_concierge_form.user_data_prefill: Name, phone, email pre-filled from user context (lines 96-105 in CelebratePage.jsx)
ask_concierge_form.pet_auto_population: Fetches user pets and auto-selects if only one pet (lines 67-93)

pawmeter_visibility.display: Shows paw icon with score/10 on all screens including mobile

service_request_user_name.verified_via: Backend API test - customer.name shows 'Dipali' not 'Guest'

pet_selector_navbar.implementation: Navbar.jsx lines 1031-1111 - dropdown to switch pets, stores in localStorage

view_details_button.implementation: ProductCard.jsx lines 322-327 - opens ProductDetailModal with full product info
